title: Track-Regime Experts Beyond Dry/Wet
summary: Undercut dominance, tyre warm-up behavior, and one-stop vs two-stop pressure vary strongly by circuit and conditions.
rationale: Undercut dominance, tyre warm-up behavior, and one-stop vs two-stop pressure vary strongly by circuit and conditions. Public F1 analyses repeatedly show Monaco-like overcut/warm-up races behave differently from high-deg undercut races, so a single global model likely averages away useful structure.
implementation_hint: Cluster `Year_Race` into a few strategy regimes using causal summaries available at prediction time or learned from prior laps in-train: degradation curvature, first-lap-after-pit delta patterns, one-stop frequency, stint-length distribution. Train expert models per regime and blend by a lightweight regime classifier.
expected_effect: Cleaner decision boundaries for races where pit timing logic is qualitatively different despite similar raw tyre ages.
risk: Only 26 races are available, so regime definitions must stay coarse to avoid fragmentation.
